NWS STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS AN EF1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 100 TO 110MPH THAT TOUCHED DOWN 4 MILES SE OF MULDROW. THIS TORNADO MOVED NE INTO PARTS OF FT. SMITH AND VAN BUREN. THE TORNADO CONTINUED EAST OF VAN BUREN. THE ENDING POINT OF THE TORNADO HAS YET TO BE DETERMINED AND WILL CONTINUE TO BE SURVEYED TODAY. THE TORNADO UPROOTED TREES, TOPPLED MULTIPLE CENTER-PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS, DAMAGED MANY HOMES AND BUSINESSES, AND SNAPPED NUMEROUS POWER POLES. THIS INFORMATION IS CONSIDERED PRELIMINARY AND WILL CONTINUE TO BE FINALIZED IN THE COMING DAYS. MORE INFORMATION CAN BE FOUND ON THIS TORNADO IN A RECENTLY ISSUED PNS.

NWS STORM SURVEY FINDS EF1 TORNADO DAMAGE FROM 1 E OF MULDROW, OK TO 3.5 W OF RENA, AR. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN AT 9:36PM AND LIFTED AT 9:47PM. THE TORNADO UPROOTED TREES, SNAPPED LARGE TREE LIMBS, SNAPPED POWER POLES, DESTROYED OUTBUILDINGS, AND SEVERAL HOMES WERE DAMAGED. A PNS PROVIDES MORE INFORMATION ON THIS TORNADO.
EM REPORTS SEVERAL TREES DOWN ACROSS THE NORTHERN PART OF CHOCTAW COUNTY INCLUDING SPENCERVILLE.
DAMAGE IN THE ROLAND AND MULDROW AREA CONSISTED OF MULTIPLE TREES DOWN. ONE TREE FELL ONTO A HOME, TRAPPING ONE PERSON INSIDE.

EM REPORTS SEVERAL HOMES RECEIVING DAMAGE WITH MULTIPLE HOMES SUSTAINING MAJOR DAMAGE. A SCHOOL AND A CHURCH ALSO RECEIVED DAMAGE. POSSIBLE TORNADO. SURVEY TEAM WILL BE SURVEYING THE AREA. TIME IS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

DELAYED REPORT: DAMAGE TO THE LINCOLN HIGH SCHOOL FOOTBALL STADIUM WAS REPORTED BY A LOCAL NEWSPAPER. THE DAMAGE INCLUDED SOME FENCING ALONG WITH THE STANDS WHERE THE BAND PLAYS. A PERSONAL WEATHER STATION LOCATED ABOUT 1 MILE TO THE SOUTHWEST REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 66MPH. LIKELY MICROBURST.

AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF BEGGS AT APPROXIMATELY 10:03PM. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED NORTHEAST A SHORT DISTANCE BEFORE LIFTING ON THE EAST SIDE OF BEGGS. THIS TORNADO DAMAGED MULTIPLE TREES AND CAUSED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES. MORE INFORMATION WILL BE RELEASED LATER WHEN THE SURVEY IS FINALIZED.
